title: Perforce 2026 State of DevOps Report: organizations with mature DevOps embed AI across 72% of SDLC vs. 18% for immature ones
summary: The Perforce 2026 State of DevOps Report, surveying 820 technology professionals globally, finds that DevOps maturity is the strongest predictor of successful AI adoption in software delivery. Organizations with high DevOps maturity embed AI across 72% of their software delivery lifecycle, compared to just 18% for low-maturity organizations. The report finds 87% of respondents believe AI will shift engineers from scripting toward system design and directing outcomes, and 55% of QA teams report increased focus on quality analytics rather than test execution. However, a significant governance gap persists: while 77% expressed confidence in AI outputs, only 39% have fully automated audit trails. Cost remains a barrier, with 37% citing cloud and energy costs as limiting factors. The report's central thesis is that incomplete DevOps is what fails, not DevOps itself, and that the organizations best positioned to benefit from AI are those that already have strong automation, testing, and delivery foundations in place.
whyItMatters: The 4x gap in AI integration (72% vs 18%) between mature and immature DevOps organizations means AI adoption may widen the performance gap between well-run and poorly-run engineering organizations rather than closing it.
The shift of 55% of QA teams from test execution to quality analytics signals a structural role transformation that engineering leaders need to plan for in hiring, training, and team design.
Only 39% having automated audit trails while 77% trust AI outputs reveals a dangerous confidence-governance gap that could expose organizations to compliance and reliability risks.
recommendedActions: Assess your organization's DevOps maturity before investing heavily in AI integration; the report suggests foundations (CI/CD, test automation, observability) are prerequisites, not afterthoughts.
Begin planning QA role evolution toward analytics, exploratory testing, and AI oversight rather than script-based test execution, which AI is increasingly handling.
Close the audit trail gap: implement automated logging and traceability for AI-assisted changes in the SDLC, especially in regulated environments where the EU AI Act will require documentation.
Factor cloud and energy costs into AI tool business cases; 37% citing cost barriers suggests current cost models may not scale with planned adoption levels.
risksAndCaveats: The survey is sponsored by Perforce, a DevOps tooling vendor, which may bias framing toward emphasizing the importance of DevOps maturity and tooling investment.
Self-reported DevOps maturity is subjective; the 72% vs 18% gap may partly reflect self-assessment bias rather than objective capability differences.
The 820-person sample may skew toward organizations that are already engaged with DevOps practices and tooling, potentially missing perspectives from organizations with no formal DevOps adoption.
